question: What are the components of the NuScale Power Module (NPM)?

nuscale_ch01_0061.title: Control Rod Drive Mechanism Proof Test
nuscale_ch01_0061.summary: The NuScale Integral System Test Program uses the NIST-1 facility, a scaled model of the NuScale Power Module (NPM), to generate thermal-hydraulic data for system characterization and validation of safety analysis codes such as NRELAP5 and PIM. These tests ensure that the unique design features and safety methodologies of the NPM are accurately represented and validated, supporting the Design Certification Application.

nuscale_ch01_0036.title: Refueling Operations
nuscale_ch01_0036.summary: Refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM) are conducted independently from other modules. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling pool, disassembling it for inspection, maintenance, and refueling, and then reassembling and returning it to service after successful testing and startup procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides an overview of the NuScale plant design, summarizing its main systems, safety features, and operational characteristics. It explains how the Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) incorporates both certified and site-specific design information, with certain details marked for context or security. The section also references figures illustrating the plant layout and system boundaries.

nuscale_ch01_0037.title: Control Building
nuscale_ch01_0037.summary: The section describes the refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM), which are conducted independently for each module. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling area using specialized cranes and tools, disassembling it for inspection, maintenance, and refueling, and then reassembling and returning it to operation after successful testing and inspection.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section explains that the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report includes simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0063.title: Emergency Core Cooling System Valve Design Certification Application Demonstration Test
nuscale_ch01_0063.summary: This section describes the demonstration test program for the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) valves in the NuScale reactor design. The program aimed to verify the functional performance of the ECCS valves—including their operation at reactor pressures and temperatures, and in borated coolant—using test articles and high-pressure test cells to simulate real operating conditions. The tests also evaluated unique design features such as the inadvertent actuation block (IAB) and confirmed the valves' operability and response.

nuscale_ch01_0002.title: Introduction
nuscale_ch01_0002.summary: This section introduces the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), which is required for standard design certification under 10 CFR 52. It outlines the scope of the report, describing the NuScale Power Module (NPM) design, its modular and scalable features, and key safety characteristics such as passive shutdown, compact containment, and advanced digital controls. The FSAR covers both individual modules and multi-module plant configurations.

nuscale_ch01_0062.title: Control Rod Assembly Drop and Control Rod Drive Shaft Alignment Test
nuscale_ch01_0062.summary: This section describes the testing and validation of the NuScale control rod assembly (CRA) drop and drive shaft alignment, focusing on performance, reliability, and repeatability. Tests conducted at Curtiss Wright facilities demonstrated that the remote disconnect mechanism reliably engages and disengages the CRA, and the results have informed improvements in the drive rod position detection circuitry, though they do not form the final design basis. The NPM features longer, remotely disconnectable drive shafts with multiple alignment supports to ensure proper operation.

edges.description: Section A discusses the use of a scaled representation of the NPM design for testing, while Section B describes operational procedures involving the physical movement and handling of the NPM, with both sections referencing the NPM as the central system of interest.

Section B provides a general description of the NPM's components, while Section A discusses testing of specific systems within the NPM, making Section A a specification or detail of the broader overview in Section B.

Section B describes operational procedures involving the movement and handling of the NuScale Power Module (NPM), thereby implying that the NPM is a distinct component, while Section A references a test program using a scaled representation of the NPM design, indicating both sections are connected by their focus on the NPM as a system component.

Section A discusses the testing and validation of the NPM's thermal-hydraulic systems, while Section B provides drawings and figures that visually clarify the design and components of the NPM, thus both sections contribute complementary information about the NPM's components—Section A through system testing and Section B through visual documentation.

Section A describes the use of a scaled test facility to replicate and analyze the integrated thermal-hydraulic phenomena of the NPM design, while Section B details specific components of the NPM, such as the emergency core cooling system valves and their locations, thus Section B provides concrete examples of NPM components referenced in Section A's general system description.

Section A describes the handling and movement of the NPM during refueling operations, while Section B provides a general overview of the plant's systems, including those related to the NPM, thus Section A provides specific operational details that elaborate on the general systems described in Section B.

Section B provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design information, including components, referenced in the general descriptions such as those in Section A's discussion of NPM refueling operations.

Section B identifies the emergency core cooling system (ECCS) valves, including reactor vent valves (RVVs), as components of the NPM, which directly addresses the analysis question about NPM components, while Section A describes refueling operations involving the movement of the NPM but does not specify its components.

Section B provides specific operational details about the handling and movement of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) during refueling, which is a subset of the general systems and operations described in Section A's overall plant description.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant's systems, including components of the NuScale Power Module, while Section B offers detailed drawings that visually specify and clarify the components described in Section A.

Section B provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design information described in Section A, including the movement and handling of the NPM during refueling operations.

Section B identifies emergency core cooling system (ECCS) valves and reactor vent valves as components of the NPM, which directly addresses the analysis question about NPM components, while Section A describes refueling operations involving the movement of the NPM but does not specify its components.

Section A provides drawings and figures that visually clarify the design and component layout of the NPM, while Section B describes specific components (ECCS valves) and their tested performance, thus Section A's visual information supports the identification and understanding of the components discussed in Section B.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its safety analysis, while Section B describes a test program that generates data specifically for characterizing and validating the NPM's systems, thus supporting the detailed understanding of its components.

Section A provides a general description of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and the scope of the safety analysis report, while Section B details a specific operational procedure (refueling) involving the NPM, thus Section B provides a specific example or detail that falls under the general description in Section A.
Section A introduces the purpose and scope of the FSAR, while Section B provides the general plant description that fulfills the introductory overview outlined in Section A.

Section A describes specific component testing (control rod assembly and drive shaft) that is part of the broader system-level testing of the NPM described in Section B, connecting individual component validation to overall system characterization.

Section A discusses testing of the control rod assembly, which is a component of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) referenced in Section B's description of NPM refueling operations, thus providing specific component detail relevant to the NPM described in Section B.
Section B provides a general overview of the plant's systems, including the control rod assembly mentioned in Section A, which is a specific component tested and described in detail.
